meaning: n.实行,完成,执行,死刑,制作,(武器等的)破坏效果,杀伤力
examples: The idea was good, but the execution was poor.
He was arrested for obstruction of a police officer in the execution of his duty.
He did not witness her execution, yet he and the others are complicit in her death.
Market pros consider 45% decent execution, partly because big selling--Gates had one 5-million-share day--depresses prices.
Perhaps it was a simple act of kindness, admirable in intent if not in execution.
